title: Max Sum Without Adjacent Elements
hints: Since we cannot choose adjacent elements (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally), for each column, we can only choose at most one element.
For each column, take the maximum value between the two rows: max(A[0][i], A[1][i]).
This reduces the problem to a 1D array where we need to maximize sum without choosing adjacent elements.
Use dynamic programming: dp[i] represents the maximum sum we can get up to column i.
For each column i, we have two choices: either take it (dp[i-2] + max_values[i]) or skip it (dp[i-1]).
Base cases: dp[0] = max_values[0], dp[1] = max(max_values[0], max_values[1]).
